Question 1: What is the most effective method to assess respiratory distress syndrome in an infant?
A. Assess for nasal flaring
B. Measure oxygen saturation
C. Assess capillary refill time
D. Measure axillary temperature
Question 2: What is an acceptable serum potassium level in an adult patient?
A. 2.8–3.2 mEq/L
B. 4.0–5.0 mEq/L
C. 5.8–6.2 mEq/L
D. 6.5–7.0 mEq/L
Question 3: Respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) is suspected in an infant. What is the nurse’s priority intervention?
A. Assess respirations and respiratory pattern
B. Administer antipyretics
C. Initiate droplet precautions
D. Encourage oral fluids
Question 4: What is the purpose of the phenylketonuria (PKU) test and when is it performed?
A. Detects hypoglycemia at birth
B. Screens metabolic disorders at delivery
C. Assesses phenylalanine metabolism at 24–48 hours after birth
D. Identifies congenital heart defects
Question 5: What is the most effective method to administer eye drop medication?
A. Directly onto the cornea
B. Inner canthus
C. Middle of the conjunctival sac
D. Eyelid margin
Question 6: What happens if the mother is Rh-positive and the infant is Rh-negative?
A. No complications occur
B. The infant develops hemolytic disease
C. Maternal antibodies attack fetal erythrocytes
D. Rhogam must be administered immediately
Question 7: What occurs when the mother is Rh-negative and the infant is Rh-positive?
A. Maternal antibodies may destroy fetal erythrocytes
B. No immune response occurs
C. Maternal blood volume decreases
D. The mother develops anemia
Question 8: Do Rh incompatibility complications worsen with subsequent pregnancies?
A. Yes, due to maternal immune memory
B. No, severity remains the same
C. No, antibodies disappear
D. Only the first pregnancy is affected
Question 9: What is the most common eating disorder?
A. Anorexia nervosa
B. Bulimia nervosa
C. Binge eating disorder
D. Pica
Question 10: How are seizures best assessed and documented?
A. Aura, incontinence, and awareness
B. Blood pressure and pulse
C. Bruising and bleeding
D. EEG findings
Question 11: What is the most common side effect of antibiotic therapy?
A. Diarrhea
B. Constipation
C. Hypertension
D. Bradycardia
Question 12: How is insulin prepared when mixing short-acting and long-acting insulin?
A. Short-acting first, long-acting second
B. Long-acting first, short-acting second
C. Either order is acceptable
D. Insulins must never be mixed
Question 13: How is pathological jaundice treated in a newborn?
A. Phototherapy with eye and genital protection
B. Increased oral feeding only
C. Antibiotic therapy
D. No treatment is required
Question 14: What is the difference between infiltration and interstitial IV complications?
A. There is no difference
B. One causes warmth and redness
C. One causes vein inflammation
D. One affects arteries
Question 15: What is the difference between phlebitis and vein inflammation?
A. There is no difference
B. One involves cold tissue
C. One causes edema without redness
D. One affects arteries
Question 16: What is the most common symptom following a craniotomy?
A. Slurred speech
B. Abdominal pain
C. Diarrhea
D. Peripheral edema
Question 17: What does the BUBBLE assessment evaluate?
A. Postpartum maternal status
B. Newborn reflexes
C. Heart failure symptoms
D. Surgical wound healing
Question 18: What is a common side effect of iron supplementation?
A. Black stools
B. White stools
C. Hematuria
D. Jaundice
Question 19: What is the best method to perform a procedure on a toddler?
A. Demonstrate using a teddy bear
B. Perform without explanation
C. Use medical terminology
D. Ask the child to observe first
Question 20: What is a common side effect of corticosteroid therapy such as prednisone?
A. Increased blood glucose levels
B. Hypoglycemia
C. Bradycardia
D. Hypotension
Question 21: When should digoxin be held?
A. Apical pulse below 70 in adults
B. Apical pulse below 60 in adults and below 90 in children
C. Systolic blood pressure below 100
D. Respiratory rate below 12
Question 22: What is the most accurate method to verify nasogastric tube placement?
A. X-ray confirmation
B. Aspirating gastric contents
C. Auscultation of air
D. Tube length measurement
Question 23: How are medications administered through a nasogastric tube?
A. Administer medications without flushing
B. Flush with cold water before medications
C. Flush with warm water before and after medications and keep patient upright
D. Place patient supine immediately after
Question 24: How should a nurse assist a patient with unilateral weakness following a CVA?
A. Assist from the stronger side only
B. Place food on the weaker side
C. Assist on the weaker side and position objects on the stronger side
D. Encourage use of the weaker side only
Question 25: Which side of the body is affected by a left-sided cerebrovascular accident?
A. Left side
B. Right side
C. Both sides equally
D. Neither side
Question 26: If digoxin and an antacid are both ordered, which should be administered first?
A. Antacid first
B. Digoxin first
C. Give together
D. Hold both medications
Question 27: What is the most serious and common side effect of Demerol?
A. Constipation
B. Nausea
C. Drowsiness
D. Respiratory depression
Question 28: What are the most common oral dosages of digoxin?
A. 0.125–0.25 mg
B. 0.5–1 mg
C. 0.0375–0.05 mg
D. 1–2 mg
Question 29: Which IV solution is used during blood transfusions?
A. D5W
B. Normal saline 0.9%
C. Normal saline 0.45%
D. Ringer’s lactate
Question 30: Which laboratory test is used to monitor warfarin therapy?
A. PTT
B. PT/INR
C. aPTT ratio
D. Platelet count
Question 31: What is the maximum daily dose of acetaminophen for an adult?
A. 4000 mg
B. 5000 mg
C. 2000 mg
D. 6000 mg
Question 32: What is atherosclerosis?
A. Hardening of the arteries
B. Buildup of lipids within an artery
C. Formation of a blood clot
D. Inflammation of veins
Question 33: What vital sign changes occur with increased intracranial pressure?
A. All vitals decrease except pulse
B. Pulse increases and BP decreases
C. Blood pressure increases while pulse and respirations decrease
D. All vitals increase
Question 34: What is used when drawing medication from an ampule?
A. Regular needle
B. Filter needle
C. Safety needle
D. Blunt needle
Question 35: What is the best diet for a patient with hyperkalemia?
A. Avoid bananas, orange juice, broccoli, and cantaloupe
B. Increase bananas and orange juice
C. High potassium diet
D. High sodium diet
Question 36: What is a normal therapeutic INR for a patient on warfarin?
A. 1–1.5
B. 2–3
C. 4–5
D. >6
Question 37: How should a wound be cleansed?
A. Distal to proximal
B. Proximal to distal using center outward technique
C. Circular motion randomly
D. From outer edges inward
Question 38: What is the preferred IM injection site for adults?
A. Deltoid
B. Ventrogluteal
C. Vastus lateralis
D. Dorsogluteal
Question 39: What is the most common side effect of beta blockers?
A. Hypertension
B. Bradycardia
C. Hyperglycemia
D. Fever
Question 40: What is the most common side effect of ACE inhibitors?
A. Bradycardia
B. Constipation
C. Persistent dry cough
D. Hypoglycemia
Question 41: What are the most common side effects of NSAIDs?
A. Gastric irritation
B. Bradycardia
C. Hypoglycemia
D. Respiratory depression
Question 42: What is the correct method to administer a fentanyl patch?
A. Apply loosely and cover with gauze
B. Press edges firmly and label with date, time, and initials
C. Apply heat over the patch
D. Cut the patch in half
Question 43: How should a bandage be applied?
A. Proximal to distal
B. Randomly based on wound size
C. Distal to proximal
D. Side to side
Question 44: What is recommended for a patient receiving postural drainage therapy?
A. Fluid restriction
B. Bed rest only
C. Oxygen restriction
D. Increased fluid intake
Question 45: How should blood pressure be taken in a patient who has had a mastectomy?
A. On the arm opposite the mastectomy
B. On the affected arm
C. On the thigh routinely
D. On both arms
Question 46: What are common symptoms of systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE)?
A. Polyuria and polydipsia
B. Joint pain, hair loss, and photosensitivity
C. Fever and productive cough
D. Weight gain and edema
Question 47: What is the most common symptom of systemic lupus erythematosus?
A. Peripheral edema
B. Joint stiffness
C. Butterfly rash on the face
D. Abdominal pain
Question 48: What is the correct intervention for increased intracranial pressure?
A. Trendelenburg position
B. Flexing the neck
C. Lying flat
D. Elevate the head of the bed 30–40 degrees
Question 49: What are the most common symptoms of multiple sclerosis?
A. Decreased sensation to pain and heat
B. Severe hypertension
C. Hyperglycemia
D. Hearing loss
Question 50: When is an LPN intervention most critical?
A. Mild hypoglycemia
B. Anaphylactic shock
C. Chronic pain
D. Constipation
Question 51: In what order is an abdominal assessment performed?
A. Inspection, palpation, percussion, auscultation
B. Palpation, percussion, auscultation, inspection
C. Inspection, auscultation, percussion, palpation
D. Auscultation, inspection, palpation, percussion
Question 52: In what order is a respiratory assessment performed?
A. Auscultation, palpation, inspection, percussion
B. Palpation, inspection, percussion, auscultation
C. Inspection, auscultation, percussion, palpation
D. Inspection, palpation, percussion, auscultation
Question 53: How is post-mortem care performed?
A. Position supine, replace dentures, provide dignity
B. Leave patient as found
C. Remove all personal items only
D. Elevate legs and restrain arms
Question 54: What is physiological jaundice?
A. Jaundice within 24 hours of birth
B. Jaundice occurring after 24 hours of birth
C. Jaundice caused by infection
D. Jaundice requiring immediate exchange transfusion
Question 55: What is pathological jaundice?
A. Jaundice after 24 hours
B. Jaundice resolving without treatment
C. Jaundice present within the first 24 hours of birth
D. Jaundice lasting less than 48 hours
Question 56: What is the best method to assess dehydration in a child?
A. Capillary refill only
B. Urine color
C. Skin temperature
D. Assess mucous membranes
Question 57: What is the best method to assess dehydration in an infant?
A. Assess fontanels for depression
B. Skin turgor
C. Urine output alone
D. Capillary refill
Question 58: When do the anterior and posterior fontanels close?
A. Both close at birth
B. Anterior at 18 months; posterior at 2–3 months
C. Anterior at 6 months; posterior at 12 months
D. Anterior at 2 months; posterior at 18 months
Question 59: What is an acceptable therapeutic PTT range for a patient receiving heparin?
A. 0.5–1
B. 1.5–2
C. 3–4
D. >5
Question 60: What is the best diet recommendation for a patient taking warfarin?
A. High intake of green leafy vegetables
B. High protein diet
C. Avoid foods high in vitamin K
D. Increase citrus fruits
Question 61: What are the stages of labor in correct sequence?
A. Dilation, delivery, placenta
B. Active, latent, transition
C. Effacement, delivery
D. Effacement and dilation, delivery of infant, delivery of placenta
Question 62: How long does lochia rubra typically last?
A. 3–5 days
B. 5–10 days
C. 2–6 weeks
D. 24 hours only
Question 63: How long does lochia serosa typically last?
A. 3–5 days
B. 5–10 days
C. 2–6 weeks
D. 12–24 hours
Question 64: How long does lochia alba typically last?
A. 3–5 days
B. 5–10 days
C. 2–6 weeks
D. 24 hours
Question 65: What is the common nickname for a patient with emphysema?
A. Blue bloater
B. Silent breather
C. Pink puffer
D. Rapid respirator
Question 66: What is the common nickname for a patient with chronic bronchitis?
A. Pink puffer
B. Blue bloater
C. Silent breather
D. Air trapper
Question 67: What does gravida 3, para 1 indicate?
A. Three pregnancies and one live birth
B. One pregnancy and three births
C. Three births and one pregnancy
D. Three living children
Question 68: What is the best treatment for breast engorgement in a postpartum mother?
A. Warm compresses continuously
B. Fluid restriction
C. Ice packs for 10–15 minutes
D. Tight binding of breasts
Question 69: What intervention helps prevent neural tube defects during pregnancy?
A. Iron supplementation only
B. Folic acid supplementation
C. Increased calcium intake
D. Vitamin C supplementation
Question 70: Where should the uterus be palpated postpartum?
A. Above the umbilicus only
B. Below the symphysis pubis
C. Below the umbilicus and above the symphysis pubis
D. At the level of the xiphoid
Question 71: What is the most appropriate care following an episiotomy?
A. Apply heat immediately
B. Perineal care back to front
C. Encourage prolonged sitting
D. Ice packs and front-to-back perineal care
Question 72: What Apgar score indicates a healthy newborn?
A. 9
B. 6
C. 3
D. 0
Question 73: What syringe and needle size is most commonly used for IM injections in adults?
A. 5/8 inch, 25 gauge
B. 1–1.5 inch, 22 gauge
C. 1/2 inch, 27 gauge
D. 3 inch, 18 gauge
Question 74: What is the correct angle for subcutaneous injections?
A. 90 degrees only
B. 15 degrees
C. 45–90 degrees
D. 5–15 degrees
Question 75: What is the preferred IM injection site for children?
A. Deltoid
B. Ventrogluteal
C. Vastus lateralis
D. Dorsogluteal
Question 76: What is the preferred IM injection technique?
A. Air-lock
B. Aspiration technique
C. Z-track method
D. Massage after injection
Question 77: How is the deltoid IM injection site landmarked?
A. Two fingerbreadths below the acromion process
B. At the midpoint of the upper arm
C. One fingerbreadth below the clavicle
D. Over the biceps muscle
Question 78: How is the ventrogluteal IM injection site landmarked?
A. Posterior superior iliac spine
B. Iliac crest only
C. Greater trochanter and iliac crest midpoint
D. Head of the greater trochanter
Question 79: What is the most common side effect of beta blockers?
A. Hypertension
B. Bradycardia
C. Hyperglycemia
D. Fever
Question 80: What is the most common side effect of ACE inhibitors?
A. Constipation
B. Hypoglycemia
C. Persistent dry cough
D. Bradycardia
Question 81: How should a wound be cleansed?
A. Distal to proximal
B. Proximal to distal, center outward
C. Random circular motion
D. Outside inward
Question 82: How should a bandage be applied?
A. Proximal to distal
B. Side to side
C. Distal to proximal
D. Randomly
Question 83: What is recommended for a patient undergoing postural drainage?
A. Fluid restriction
B. Strict bed rest
C. Oxygen restriction
D. Increased fluid intake
Question 84: What is the correct method to take blood pressure in a patient with a mastectomy?
A. On the arm opposite the mastectomy
B. On the affected arm
C. On the thigh routinely
D. On both arms
Question 85: What is the most common symptom of systemic lupus erythematosus?
A. Peripheral edema
B. Joint stiffness
C. Butterfly rash on the face
D. Abdominal pain
Question 86: What is the correct intervention for increased intracranial pressure?
A. Trendelenburg position
B. Neck flexion
C. Lying flat
D. Elevate head of bed 30–40°
Question 87: What is the most common symptom of multiple sclerosis?
A. Decreased sensation to pain and heat
B. Severe hypertension
C. Hyperglycemia
D. Hearing loss
Question 88: When is an LPN intervention most critical?
A. Mild hypoglycemia
B. Anaphylactic shock
C. Chronic pain
D. Constipation
Question 89: What is the correct sequence for an abdominal assessment?
A. Inspection, palpation, percussion, auscultation
B. Palpation, percussion, auscultation, inspection
C. Inspection, auscultation, percussion, palpation
D. Auscultation, inspection, palpation, percussion
Question 90: How is a respiratory assessment performed?
A. Auscultation, palpation, inspection, percussion
B. Palpation, inspection, percussion, auscultation
C. Inspection, auscultation, percussion, palpation
D. Inspection, palpation, percussion, auscultation
Question 91: How is post-mortem care performed?
A. Supine positioning, hygiene, dignity, family viewing
B. Leave patient untouched
C. Remove lines only
D. Elevate legs and restrain arms
Question 92: What defines physiological jaundice in a newborn?
A. Jaundice within 24 hours of birth
B. Jaundice appearing after 24 hours of birth
C. Jaundice caused by infection
D. Jaundice requiring immediate exchange transfusion
Question 93: What defines pathological jaundice in a newborn?
A. Jaundice after 24 hours
B. Jaundice resolving without treatment
C. Jaundice present within the first 24 hours
D. Jaundice lasting less than 48 hours
Question 94: What is the best method to assess dehydration in a child?
A. Capillary refill only
B. Urine color
C. Skin temperature
D. Assess mucous membranes
Question 95: What is the best method to assess dehydration in an infant?
A. Assess fontanels for depression
B. Skin turgor
C. Urine output only
D. Capillary refill
Question 96: When do the anterior and posterior fontanels close?
A. Both close at birth
B. Anterior at 18 months; posterior at 2–3 months
C. Anterior at 6 months; posterior at 12 months
D. Anterior at 2 months; posterior at 18 months
Question 97: What is the most common side effect of NSAIDs?
A. Gastric irritation
B. Bradycardia
C. Hypoglycemia
D. Respiratory depression
Question 98: What is the correct method to administer a fentanyl patch?
A. Cut the patch in half
B. Press edges firmly and label with date, time, and initials
C. Apply heat over the patch
D. Apply loosely
Question 99: What is the priority nursing assessment for a patient receiving opioid analgesics?
A. Respiratory rate
B. Blood pressure
C. Pain score
D. Bowel sounds
Question 100: Which finding indicates digoxin toxicity?
A. Constipation
B. Yellow-green visual halos
C. Hypertension
D. Polyuria
Question 101: Which electrolyte imbalance places a patient at greatest risk for cardiac dysrhythmias?
A. Hyponatremia
B. Hypocalcemia
C. Hyperkalemia
D. Hypomagnesemia
Question 102: Which assessment finding suggests fluid volume overload?
A. Dry mucous membranes
B. Decreased urine output
C. Orthostatic hypotension
D. Crackles in lung bases
Question 103: Which action best prevents the spread of Clostridioides difficile?
A. Handwashing with soap and water
B. Alcohol-based hand sanitizer
C. Wearing a mask
D. Negative-pressure room
Question 104: What is the priority intervention for a patient experiencing hypoglycemia?
A. Administer long-acting insulin
B. Provide a fast-acting carbohydrate
C. Encourage exercise
D. Check HbA1c
Question 105: Which position best reduces aspiration risk during enteral feeding?
A. Supine
B. Trendelenburg
C. Head of bed elevated 30–45°
D. Side-lying flat
Question 106: Which sign indicates early hypoxia?
A. Cyanosis
B. Hypotension
C. Bradycardia
D. Restlessness
Question 107: Which intervention best prevents pressure injuries?
A. Repositioning every two hours
B. Massage over bony prominences
C. Applying lotion only
D. Limiting fluids
Question 108: Which vital sign change is most concerning in a patient with sepsis?
A. Bradycardia
B. Hypotension
C. Fever
D. Mild tachypnea
Question 109: Which medication requires monitoring for ototoxicity?
A. Acetaminophen
B. Metformin
C. Gentamicin
D. Amoxicillin
Question 110: Which finding suggests urinary retention?
A. Concentrated urine
B. Dysuria
C. Cloudy urine
D. Bladder distension
Question 111: Which intervention is most effective in preventing deep vein thrombosis?
A. Early ambulation
B. Warm compresses
C. Fluid restriction
D. Massage of calves
Question 112: Which symptom is most concerning in a patient with asthma?
A. Mild wheezing
B. Cough
C. Silent chest
D. Tachycardia
Question 113: Which finding indicates effective oxygen therapy?
A. Increased heart rate
B. Improved oxygen saturation
C. Warm skin
D. Increased appetite
Question 114: Which action reduces the risk of catheter-associated urinary tract infection?
A. Routine catheter irrigation
B. Disconnecting the system frequently
C. Maintaining a closed drainage system
D. Keeping the bag above bladder level
Question 115: Which finding suggests fluid volume deficit?
A. Bounding pulse
B. Peripheral edema
C. Tachycardia
D. Crackles
Question 116: Which action best promotes sleep for a hospitalized patient?
A. Frequent nighttime vital signs
B. Clustering nursing care
C. Bright lighting
D. Keeping the television on
Question 117: Which intervention best prevents aspiration in a patient with dysphagia?
A. Thickened liquids
B. Thin liquids
C. Supine positioning
D. Large bolus feeding
Question 118: Which finding indicates adequate pain control?
A. Drowsiness
B. Stable vital signs only
C. Relaxed facial expression
D. Patient reports pain relief
Question 119: Which assessment finding is most concerning in a patient receiving opioid analgesics?
A. Respiratory rate of 10/min
B. Mild drowsiness
C. Constipation
D. Nausea
Question 120: Which laboratory value requires immediate follow-up in a patient receiving digoxin?
A. Potassium 4.2 mmol/L
B. Digoxin level 2.6 ng/mL
C. Sodium 138 mmol/L
D. Calcium 2.3 mmol/L
Question 121: Which patient is at highest risk for developing hypoglycemia?
A. Patient taking corticosteroids
B. Patient with infection
C. Patient who skipped a meal after insulin
D. Patient receiving TPN
Question 122: Which sign is an early indicator of shock?
A. Hypotension
B. Cyanosis
C. Tachycardia
D. Altered level of consciousness
Question 123: Which intervention is most appropriate for a patient with hyperkalemia?
A. Encourage potassium-rich foods
B. Restrict sodium intake
C. Increase oral fluids only
D. Administer insulin with glucose as ordered
Question 124: Which intervention best reduces the risk of falls in older adults?
A. Ensuring adequate lighting
B. Keeping bed in high position
C. Wearing loose footwear
D. Physical restraints
Question 125: Which assessment finding indicates dehydration?
A. Bounding pulse
B. Poor skin turgor
C. Crackles
D. Peripheral edema
Question 126: Which patient finding suggests worsening asthma?
A. Mild wheezing
B. Increased cough
C. Use of accessory muscles
D. Cyanosis
Question 127: Which action best prevents aspiration in a patient receiving tube feedings?
A. Lying flat
B. Rapid infusion
C. Checking residuals only
D. Elevating head of bed 30–45°
Question 128: Which assessment finding suggests infection?
A. Elevated white blood cell count
B. Fatigue
C. Bradycardia
D. Concentrated urine
Question 129: Which intervention best promotes bowel elimination?
A. Bed rest
B. Increased fiber intake
C. Fluid restriction
D. Suppressing urge to defecate
Question 130: Which vital sign change is most concerning in dehydration?
A. Mild fever
B. Hypotension
C. Tachycardia
D. Bradycardia
Question 131: Which action reduces the risk of catheter-associated urinary tract infection?
A. Routine catheter irrigation
B. Frequent disconnection
C. Maintaining a closed drainage system
D. Placing bag above bladder
Question 132: Which assessment finding suggests fluid volume overload?
A. Dry mucous membranes
B. Orthostatic hypotension
C. Peripheral edema
D. Tachycardia
Question 133: Which intervention best prevents pressure injuries?
A. Repositioning every two hours
B. Massaging bony prominences
C. Applying lotion only
D. Limiting fluid intake
Question 134: Which finding indicates adequate oxygenation?
A. Tachypnea
B. Oxygen saturation of 96%
C. Warm skin
D. Increased appetite
Question 135: Which patient statement indicates effective pain management?
A. “I feel sleepy.”
B. “I can rest.”
C. “I look relaxed.”
D. “My pain is much better.”
Question 136: Which intervention best promotes sleep in hospitalized patients?
A. Frequent nighttime assessments
B. Clustering nursing care
C. Bright lighting
D. Keeping television on
Question 137: Which finding indicates early hypoxia?
A. Cyanosis
B. Hypotension
C. Bradycardia
D. Restlessness
Question 138: Which intervention best prevents deep vein thrombosis?
A. Early ambulation
B. Warm compresses
C. Fluid restriction
D. Massaging calves
Question 139: What is the correct method to chart nursing care?
A. Factual, organized, concise, accurate, and legible
B. Detailed and narrative
C. Opinion-based
D. Informal and brief
Question 140: What is the correct administration of nitroglycerin spray for angina?
A. Spray into the back of the throat
B. 0.3–0.4 mg sublingually every 5 minutes up to 3 doses
C. One spray every hour
D. Swallow immediately
Question 141: How often should a tracheostomy dressing be changed?
A. Once daily
B. Every 4 hours
C. Once per shift
D. Only if visibly soiled
Question 142: What is the priority preparation for a patient with a suspected myocardial infarction?
A. Obtain dietary history
B. Place the patient on bed rest only
C. Prepare discharge paperwork
D. Ensure crash cart readiness and clear hallways
Question 143: What is the safest intervention for broken medical equipment?
A. Label as out of order and submit maintenance requisition
B. Continue using with caution
C. Store in patient room
D. Discard immediately
Question 144: What must be confirmed before inserting a urinary catheter?
A. Room availability
B. Physician order and patient consent
C. Catheter size only
D. Dietary intake
Question 145: Where should a urinary catheter drainage bag be positioned?
A. On the bed
B. At bladder level
C. Below bladder level
D. Hung on IV pole
Question 146: What is the most common catheter size used for adult males?
A. 16–20 French
B. 10–12 French
C. 8–10 French
D. 22–24 French
Question 147: What is the most common catheter size used for adult females?
A. 18–22 French
B. 12–16 French
C. 8–10 French
D. 20–24 French
Question 148: What is the most important principle to follow during any clinical procedure?
A. Personal preference
B. Past experience only
C. Facility policy, procedure, and protocol
D. Peer advice only
Question 149: What is the safest approach when performing a procedure for the first time?
A. Perform independently
B. Review policy and have an experienced nurse observe
C. Refuse the procedure
D. Watch a video only
Question 150: What is a common side effect of lithium therapy?
A. Hypoglycemia
B. Bradycardia
C. Sodium retention
D. Diarrhea
Question 151: What best describes sundowning?
A. Behavioral changes in dementia patients during evening hours
B. Acute psychosis
C. Sleep apnea
D. Depression
Question 152: What is the most effective intervention for managing sundowning?
A. Bright lighting
B. Use of a nightlight and low bed position
C. Physical restraints
D. Frequent room changes
Question 153: What clarification should be asked when a restraint is ordered?
A. Duration only
B. Location only
C. Type of restraint to be used
D. Family preference
Question 154: What is pseudomenstruation in a newborn?
A. Vaginal bleeding due to trauma
B. Bleeding caused by infection
C. Hormone-related vaginal bleeding in newborn girls
D. Bleeding requiring intervention
Question 155: What precaution is required for Hepatitis A, D, and E?
A. Gloves only
B. Hand hygiene
C. Airborne precautions
D. N95 mask
Question 156: What precaution is required for Hepatitis B and C?
A. Hand hygiene only
B. Droplet precautions
C. Airborne isolation
D. Gloves when handling bodily fluids
Question 157: When should a Jackson-Pratt drain be emptied?
A. When it is half full
B. When completely full
C. Every hour
D. Only if leaking
Question 158: Which finding suggests infection in a Jackson-Pratt drain?
A. Serous drainage
B. Serosanguineous fluid
C. Light pink drainage
D. Brown-colored drainage
Question 159: What is the priority assessment after administering IV morphine?
A. Respiratory rate
B. Blood pressure
C. Pain score
D. Bowel sounds
Question 160: Which finding indicates hypocalcemia?
A. Decreased deep tendon reflexes
B. Positive Trousseau sign
C. Bradycardia
D. Polyuria
Question 161: Which patient is at highest risk for aspiration?
A. Alert patient eating independently
B. Patient with controlled GERD
C. Stroke patient with dysphagia
D. Patient on a soft diet
Question 162: Which intervention is most appropriate for a patient with hyponatremia?
A. Encourage free water intake
B. Administer potassium supplements
C. Rapid IV fluids
D. Fluid restriction as ordered
Question 163: Which finding indicates effective asthma management?
A. Decreased wheezing
B. Increased use of rescue inhaler
C. Use of accessory muscles
D. Silent chest
Question 164: Which intervention best reduces the risk of ventilator-associated pneumonia?
A. Supine positioning
B. Head of bed elevation 30–45°
C. Routine suctioning
D. Fluid restriction
Question 165: Which assessment finding indicates early sepsis?
A. Hypotension
B. Altered level of consciousness
C. Tachycardia
D. Oliguria
Question 166: Which action best prevents medication errors?
A. Following the rights of medication administration
B. Relying on memory
C. Preparing medications in advance
D. Skipping double checks
Question 167: Which finding suggests urinary tract infection?
A. Dark urine
B. Fatigue
C. Burning with urination
D. Edema
Question 168: Which intervention best manages nausea?
A. Large meals
B. Small, frequent meals
C. Lying flat after eating
D. Forcing fluids rapidly
Question 169: Which sign indicates worsening heart failure?
A. Stable weight
B. Mild fatigue
C. Sudden weight gain
D. Dry skin

A. Supine
B. High Fowler’s
C. Trendelenburg
D. Prone

A. Excessive bruising
B. Therapeutic INR range
C. Hematuria
D. Epistaxis

A. Involving the patient in care decisions
B. Making decisions for the patient
C. Limiting patient choices
D. Withholding information

A. Fine tremor
B. Mild thirst
C. Slurred speech
D. Increased urination

B. Calm environment with consistent routine
C. Physical restraints
D. Bright lighting at night

B. Reusing supplies

B. Diaphoresis
C. Warm, dry skin
D. Fruity breath

A. Increased urine output
B. Clear urine
C. Rising creatinine level
D. Normal blood pressure

A. Signing forms without explanation
B. Pressuring the patient to agree
C. Withholding risks
D. Ensuring understanding and voluntary agreement

B. Administer oral analgesics
C. Obtain a dietary history
D. Encourage ambulation

A. Mild incisional pain
B. Sudden shortness of breath
C. Decreased appetite

C. MMR
D. Hepatitis B
Question 343: A patient develops itching during a blood transfusion. What is the likely cause?
A. Urticaria
B. Hemolytic reaction
C. Septic reaction
D. Circulatory overload
Question 344: What is the most important parameter to monitor after administering nitroglycerin?
A. Heart rate
B. Blood pressure
C. Oxygen saturation
D. Urine output
Question 345: Which symptoms are most consistent with digoxin toxicity?
A. Tremors and diaphoresis
B. Chest pain and dyspnea
C. Nausea, anorexia, and blurred vision
D. Hypotension and flushing
Question 346: What intervention best enhances iron absorption?
A. Administer with milk
B. Administer with meals
C. Administer at bedtime
D. Administer with orange juice
Question 347: What is the primary purpose of hydrochlorothiazide?
A. Treat hypertension
B. Spare potassium
C. Treat heart failure only
D. Increase sodium levels
Question 348: Which antacid is most commonly prescribed for patients with CHF receiving furosemide?
A. Calcium carbonate
B. Ranitidine
C. Omeprazole
D. Aluminum hydroxide
Question 349: What is the most important intervention for a patient with scabies?
A. Apply topical steroids
B. Isolate for 24 hours
C. Change bed linens daily
D. Administer antihistamines only
Question 350: What tasks should never be delegated according to the EAT rule?
A. Transporting patients
B. Vital sign collection
C. Bed making
D. Evaluation, assessment, and teaching
Question 351: Which assessment finding is most concerning in a postoperative patient?
A. Sudden drop in blood pressure
B. Mild incisional pain
C. Decreased appetite
D. Fatigue
Question 352: Which nursing action best prevents atelectasis in a postoperative patient?
A. Limiting mobility
B. Encouraging incentive spirometry
C. Restricting fluids
D. Maintaining supine positioning
Question 353: Which finding indicates hypocalcemia?
A. Decreased deep tendon reflexes
B. Polyuria
C. Muscle twitching
D. Hypotension
Question 354: Which intervention is most appropriate for a patient with hyperglycemia?
A. Encourage sugary fluids
B. Withhold insulin
C. Administer glucagon
D. Administer insulin as prescribed
Question 355: Which assessment finding indicates fluid volume deficit?
A. Tachycardia
B. Peripheral edema
C. Crackles
D. Bounding pulse
Question 356: Which nursing action best prevents surgical site infection?
A. Reusing sterile gloves
B. Leaving dressings open
C. Performing hand hygiene before wound care
D. Delaying dressing changes

A. Stable weight
B. Mild fatigue
C. Sudden weight gain
D. Dry skin

A. Massaging bony prominences
B. Repositioning every two hours
C. Applying lotion only
D. Limiting fluid intake

A. Polyuria
B. Warm, dry skin
C. Diaphoresis
D. Fruity breath

C. Bradycardia

A. Signing forms without explanation

D. Rising white blood cell count

D. Peripheral edema

D. Fluid restriction
Question 523: Which assessment finding indicates infection?
A. Elevated white blood cell count
B. Fatigue
C. Bradycardia
D. Concentrated urine
Question 524: Which action best supports informed consent?
A. Signing forms without explanation
B. Pressuring the patient
C. Withholding risks
D. Ensuring understanding and voluntary agreement
Question 525: Which finding indicates effective pain management?
A. Patient reports pain relief
B. Drowsiness
C. Relaxed posture
D. Stable vital signs
Question 526: Which intervention best reduces anxiety in hospitalized patients?
A. Providing clear explanations
B. Withholding information
C. Ignoring concerns
D. Minimizing questions
Question 527: Which finding indicates effective anticoagulation with warfarin?
A. Excessive bruising
B. Therapeutic INR level
C. Hematuria
D. Epistaxis
Question 528: Which nursing action best promotes patient safety?
A. Using two patient identifiers
B. Relying on memory
C. Skipping safety checks
D. Assuming orders are correct
Question 529: Which finding indicates worsening infection?
A. Low-grade fever
B. Fatigue
C. Mild redness
D. Rising white blood cell count
Question 530: Which assessment finding suggests dehydration?
A. Bounding pulse
B. Poor skin turgor
C. Crackles
D. Peripheral edema
Question 531: Which assessment finding is most concerning in a patient with suspected gastrointestinal bleeding?
A. Black, tarry stools
B. Mild abdominal discomfort
C. Decreased appetite
D. Occasional nausea
Question 532: Which nursing action best reduces aspiration risk for a patient receiving enteral feedings?
A. Laying the patient flat
B. Elevating the head of the bed 30–45°
C. Administering feedings rapidly
D. Flushing the tube only after feeding
Question 533: Which finding indicates hyponatremia?
A. Thirst and dry mucous membranes
B. Bounding pulse
C. Confusion and headache
D. Muscle weakness with flaccidity
Question 534: Which intervention is most appropriate for metabolic alkalosis?
A. Encouraging hyperventilation
B. Restricting fluids
C. Administering sodium bicarbonate
D. Treating the underlying cause
Question 535: Which assessment finding indicates fluid volume deficit?
A. Decreased urine output
B. Jugular vein distention
C. Peripheral edema
D. Crackles in lung bases
Question 536: Which nursing action best prevents medication errors?
A. Preparing medications early
B. Relying on memory
C. Verifying patient identity with two identifiers
D. Skipping allergy checks
Question 537: Which finding suggests worsening chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD)?
A. Clear lung sounds
B. Oxygen saturation of 96%
C. Increased use of accessory muscles
D. Decreased cough
Question 538: Which intervention best prevents pressure injuries?
A. Applying powder to skin
B. Repositioning every two hours
C. Massaging reddened areas
D. Limiting protein intake
Question 539: Which assessment finding indicates hypoglycemia?
A. Polyuria
B. Warm, dry skin
C. Tremors and sweating
D. Fruity breath
Question 540: Which nursing action best supports patient autonomy?
A. Respecting informed patient decisions
B. Making decisions without consultation
C. Withholding information
D. Pressuring consent
Question 541: Which finding indicates worsening renal function?
A. Increased urine output
B. Clear urine
C. Rising serum creatinine
D. Stable blood pressure
Question 542: Which intervention best prevents deep vein thrombosis?
A. Early ambulation
B. Applying heat to legs
C. Massaging calves
D. Fluid restriction
Question 543: Which assessment finding indicates infection?
A. Elevated white blood cell count
B. Bradycardia
C. Fatigue
D. Concentrated urine
Question 544: Which action best supports informed consent?
A. Signing forms without explanation
B. Pressuring the patient
C. Withholding risks
D. Ensuring understanding and voluntary agreement
Question 545: Which finding indicates effective pain management?
A. Patient reports pain relief
B. Drowsiness
C. Relaxed posture
D. Stable vital signs
Question 546: Which intervention best reduces anxiety in hospitalized patients?
A. Providing clear explanations
B. Withholding information
C. Ignoring concerns
D. Minimizing questions
Question 547: Which finding indicates effective anticoagulation with warfarin?
A. Excessive bruising
B. Therapeutic INR level
C. Hematuria
D. Epistaxis
Question 548: Which nursing action best promotes patient safety?
A. Using two patient identifiers
B. Relying on memory
C. Skipping safety checks
D. Assuming orders are correct
Question 549: Which finding indicates worsening infection?
A. Low-grade fever
B. Fatigue
C. Mild redness
D. Rising white blood cell count
Question 550: Which assessment finding suggests dehydration?
A. Bounding pulse
B. Poor skin turgor
C. Crackles
D. Peripheral edema
Question 551: What is the best intervention to teach a child about a medical procedure?
A. Explain the procedure in detail using medical terminology
B. Use a doll or teddy bear to demonstrate the procedure
C. Ask the parents to leave the room
D. Provide written instructions
Question 552: What is the most simple balanced meal that aligns with Canada’s Food Guide?
A. Peanut butter sandwich, an orange, and a glass of skim milk
B. Cereal with whole milk only
C. Pasta with butter
D. Soup and crackers
Question 553: What is the best response to a patient who is embarrassed after being found following an accident?
A. Reassure them that accidents happen
B. Change the subject
C. Ask if they are having suicidal thoughts
D. Tell them not to feel embarrassed
Question 554: What is the correct method for testing tuberculosis?
A. Subcutaneous injection with immediate reading
B. Oral blood test
C. Intramuscular injection with same-day reading
D. Intradermal injection read in 24–48 hours
Question 555: What is the best question to ask a patient experiencing dyspnea at rest?
A. “Do you have a family history of emphysema?”
B. “How long have you been feeling anxious?”
C. “Did you exercise today?”
D. “Do you feel tired?”
Question 556: What factor is most commonly misunderstood between nurses and patients?
A. Education level
B. Cultural and religious beliefs
C. Language proficiency
D. Health literacy
Question 557: What must be done after checking blood glucose but before drawing up insulin?
A. Clean the injection site
B. Roll the insulin vial
C. Check the expiration date of the insulin
D. Verify the patient’s identity
Question 558: What is the appropriate nursing action when a patient with an STI has a spouse?
A. Notify the spouse directly
B. Contact public health immediately
C. Inform the spouse anonymously
D. Encourage the patient to inform their spouse
Question 559: What is the priority intervention for a home-care patient displaying polypharmacy?
A. Educate about dangers of mixing unreviewed medications
B. Discard all expired medications immediately
C. Report to pharmacy services
D. Ask family to manage medications
Question 560: What is the best teaching point for a pregnant patient who does not eat animal protein?
A. Animal protein is required during pregnancy
B. Many plant-based foods provide adequate protein
C. Supplements must replace all protein
D. Diet changes are unnecessary
Question 561: What is the best intervention for palliative pain management?
A. PRN oral analgesics only
B. Delay analgesia until pain increases
C. Request a patient-controlled analgesia pump
D. Use non-pharmacological methods only
Question 562: A post-operative patient reports abdominal pressure with a catheter in place. What is the priority action?
A. Irrigate the catheter immediately
B. Remove the catheter
C. Check catheter drainage for obstruction
D. Increase IV fluids
Question 563: What is the best intervention when family members fear patient deterioration after discharge?
A. Arrange a meeting with social work or home care
B. Reassure them without action
C. Delay discharge indefinitely
D. Advise them to monitor independently
Question 564: A patient has black stools, tachycardia, and restlessness. What is the priority intervention?
A. Reassess in 30 minutes
B. Inform the physician immediately
C. Administer iron
D. Encourage oral fluids
Question 565: What is the most appropriate communication approach with an adolescent diagnosed with a terminal illness?
A. Provide detailed prognostic statistics
B. Avoid discussing the diagnosis
C. Direct all communication to parents
D. Acknowledge the diagnosis and allow the adolescent to initiate discussion
Question 566: What is the best response to a patient who reports hearing voices?
A. Acknowledge and ask what the voices are saying
B. Tell the patient the voices are not real
C. Ignore the comment
D. Reassure without assessment
Question 567: What is the best initial step when teaching a group of patients?
A. Begin lecturing immediately
B. Provide written materials
C. Assess group and individual learning needs
D. Ask the most knowledgeable patient to lead
Question 568: What dietary intervention best supports healing of an advanced pressure ulcer in a geriatric patient?
A. Low-protein diet
B. Diet including yogurt, fruit, and nuts
C. Clear fluids only
D. High-sodium diet
Question 569: What is the priority intervention for a patient with oxygen saturation below 80%?
A. Obtain a chest x-ray
B. Encourage deep breathing
C. Reassess in 15 minutes
D. Administer oxygen therapy
Question 570: In prostate cancer, which laboratory value is most commonly elevated?
A. Prostate-specific antigen
B. Creatinine
C. Hemoglobin
D. White blood cell count
Question 571: What is the priority nursing assessment for an older adult who has fallen at home?
A. Level of consciousness and neurological status
B. Medication compliance
C. Home environment layout
D. Nutritional intake
Question 572: Which nursing action best supports a patient experiencing acute grief?
A. Encouraging the patient to remain positive
B. Sitting quietly and allowing expression of feelings
C. Changing the subject
D. Providing advice immediately
Question 573: Which finding is most consistent with dehydration in an infant?
A. Bulging fontanelle
B. Increased urine output
C. Sunken fontanelle
D. Moist mucous membranes
Question 574: What is the most appropriate intervention for a patient experiencing a panic attack?
A. Leave the patient alone
B. Provide extensive explanations
C. Encourage decision-making
D. Stay with the patient and speak calmly
Question 575: Which assessment finding indicates fluid volume overload?
A. Jugular vein distention
B. Dry mucous membranes
C. Poor skin turgor
D. Decreased urine output
Question 576: Which intervention best reduces the risk of aspiration in a patient with dysphagia?
A. Offering thin liquids
B. Encouraging large bites
C. Positioning upright during meals
D. Feeding quickly
Question 577: Which laboratory finding indicates anemia?
A. Elevated white blood cell count
B. Elevated platelet count
C. Low hemoglobin level
D. Increased hematocrit
Question 578: Which nursing action best prevents pressure injuries in an immobile patient?
A. Applying heat to bony areas
B. Repositioning at least every two hours
C. Massaging reddened skin
D. Limiting fluid intake
Question 579: Which finding is most indicative of hypoglycemia?
A. Polyuria
B. Warm, dry skin
C. Tremors and diaphoresis
D. Fruity breath
Question 580: Which nursing action best supports patient dignity during personal care?
A. Closing curtains and doors
B. Completing care quickly
C. Discussing care with others present
D. Ignoring patient preferences
Question 581: Which finding suggests worsening renal function?
A. Increased urine output
B. Clear urine
C. Rising serum creatinine
D. Stable blood pressure
Question 582: Which intervention best prevents deep vein thrombosis in a hospitalized patient?
A. Early ambulation
B. Applying heat to legs
C. Massaging calves
D. Restricting fluids

A. Elevated white blood cell count
B. Fatigue
C. Bradycardia
D. Concentrated urine

A. Signing forms without explanation
B. Pressuring the patient
C. Withholding risks
D. Ensuring understanding and voluntary agreement

A. Patient reports pain relief
B. Drowsiness
C. Relaxed posture
D. Stable vital signs

A. Providing clear explanations

D. Minimizing questions

A. Excessive bruising
B. Therapeutic INR level
C. Hematuria
D. Epistaxis

A. Using two patient identifiers
B. Relying on memory
C. Skipping safety checks
D. Assuming orders are correct

A. Low-grade fever
B. Fatigue
C. Mild redness
D. Rising white blood cell count
Question 590: Which assessment finding suggests dehydration?
A. Bounding pulse
B. Poor skin turgor
C. Crackles
D. Peripheral edema
Question 591: What is the priority nursing action for a patient with suspected meningitis?
A. Implement droplet precautions immediately
B. Provide oral fluids
C. Lower environmental stimulation
D. Reassess in one hour
Question 592: Which nursing intervention best supports a patient experiencing delirium?
A. Frequently changing caregivers
B. Reorienting the patient regularly
C. Keeping lights off at all times
D. Encouraging isolation
Question 593: Which assessment finding is most consistent with iron-deficiency anemia?
A. Flushed skin
B. Hypertension
C. Fatigue and pallor
D. Weight gain
Question 594: What is the most appropriate action for a patient experiencing a febrile seizure?
A. Restrain the child
B. Insert an oral airway
C. Lower body temperature rapidly with ice
D. Place the child on their side and protect from injury
Question 595: Which assessment finding indicates fluid volume deficit?
A. Tachycardia
B. Jugular vein distention
C. Peripheral edema
D. Crackles in lungs
Question 596: Which nursing action best prevents falls in hospitalized older adults?
A. Keeping bed in highest position
B. Limiting mobility
C. Ensuring call bell is within reach
D. Applying physical restraints
Question 597: Which finding suggests worsening asthma in a pediatric patient?
A. Productive cough
B. Oxygen saturation of 98%
C. Decreased peak expiratory flow
D. Clear breath sounds
Question 598: Which intervention best prevents pressure injuries in a bedbound patient?
A. Applying heat to bony areas
B. Repositioning at least every two hours
C. Massaging reddened skin
D. Limiting protein intake
Question 599: Which sign is most indicative of hypoglycemia?
A. Polyuria
B. Warm, dry skin
C. Diaphoresis and tremors
D. Fruity breath
Question 600: Which nursing action best supports patient dignity during hospitalization?
A. Involving the patient in care decisions
B. Making decisions without consultation
C. Rushing care tasks
D. Discussing care publicly
Question 601: Which laboratory finding indicates worsening renal failure?
A. Increased urine output
B. Clear urine
C. Rising serum creatinine
D. Stable blood pressure
Question 602: Which intervention best prevents deep vein thrombosis after surgery?
A. Early ambulation
B. Applying warm compresses
C. Massaging calves
D. Restricting fluids

A. Elevated white blood cell count
B. Fatigue
C. Bradycardia
D. Concentrated urine

A. Obtaining a signature only
B. Explaining benefits only
C. Asking family to decide
D. Verifying understanding of risks and alternatives

A. Patient reports pain relief
B. Drowsiness
C. Relaxed posture

D. Rising white blood cell count
Question 610: Which assessment finding suggests dehydration?
A. Bounding pulse
B. Poor skin turgor
C. Crackles
D. Peripheral edema
Question 611: What is the priority nursing action for a patient with suspected stroke symptoms?
A. Activate the stroke protocol immediately
B. Provide oral fluids
C. Reassess in 30 minutes
D. Obtain a detailed health history
Question 612: Which nursing intervention best supports a patient with acute confusion?
A. Frequently changing routines
B. Providing frequent reorientation
C. Keeping the room dark
D. Encouraging isolation
Question 613: Which assessment finding is most consistent with hypovolemic shock?
A. Bounding pulse
B. Hypertension
C. Cool, clammy skin
D. Slow capillary refill

A. Insert an oral airway
B. Restrain the patient
C. Place the patient supine
D. Protect the patient from injury

A. Decreased urine output
B. Jugular vein distention
C. Peripheral edema
D. Crackles in lungs

A. Keeping side rails raised at all times
B. Limiting patient mobility
C. Ensuring non-slip footwear is worn
D. Dim lighting during the day

A. Stable weight
B. Clear lung sounds
C. Sudden weight gain

A. Administer oxygen and assess vital signs
B. Obtain a detailed pain history
C. Encourage the patient to rest
D. Reassess in 15 minutes
Question 632: Which nursing intervention best supports a patient experiencing acute anxiety?
A. Providing extensive teaching
B. Remaining with the patient and speaking calmly
C. Leaving the patient alone

A. Jugular vein distention
B. Peripheral edema
C. Dry mucous membranes
D. Bounding pulse

A. Administer insulin
B. Encourage exercise

D. Provide a fast-acting carbohydrate

A. Crackles in lung bases
B. Poor skin turgor
C. Dry mucous membranes
D. Decreased urine output

D. Rising white blood cell count

A. Bounding pulse
B. Poor skin turgor
C. Crackles
D. Peripheral edema

A. Initiate prescribed sepsis protocol
B. Encourage oral fluid intake
C. Provide comfort measures only
D. Reassess in one hour

A. Providing complex explanations
B. Remaining with the patient and speaking calmly
C. Leaving the patient alone
D. Encouraging independent decision-making

A. Bounding pulse
B. Hypertension
C. Cool, clammy skin
D. Warm extremities

A. Insert an oral airway

A. Decreased urine output
B. Jugular vein distention
C. Peripheral edema
D. Crackles in lung bases

A. Keeping side rails raised at all times

D. Reducing lighting levels

A. Stable weight
B. Clear lung sounds
C. Rapid weight gain
D. Dry mucous membranes

A. Applying heat to bony areas
B. Repositioning every two hours
C. Massaging reddened skin
D. Limiting protein intake

A. Polyuria
B. Warm, dry skin
C. Tremors and diaphoresis
D. Fruity breath

A. Frequently changing staff assignments

A. Bounding pulse

Question 1371: Which action prevents infection spread?
A. Reuse gloves
B. Hand hygiene
C. Skip PPE
D. Delay cleaning
Question 1372: Which finding best indicates effective pain management?
A. Drowsiness
B. Patient reports pain relief
C. Relaxed posture
D. Stable vital signs
Question 1373: Which intervention best reduces anxiety before a diagnostic procedure?
A. Withholding information
B. Providing clear explanations
C. Minimizing questions
D. Encouraging silence
Question 1374: Which finding indicates effective anticoagulation with warfarin?
A. Excessive bruising
B. Therapeutic INR
C. Hematuria
D. Epistaxis
Question 1375: Which nursing action best promotes patient safety?
A. Relying on memory
B. Using two patient identifiers
C. Skipping safety checks
D. Assuming orders are correct
Question 1376: Which finding indicates worsening infection?
A. Low-grade fever
B. Fatigue
C. Mild redness
D. Rising white blood cell count
Question 1377: Which assessment finding suggests dehydration?
A. Bounding pulse
B. Poor skin turgor
C. Crackles
D. Peripheral edema
Question 1378: Which finding indicates fluid volume excess?
A. Dry mucous membranes
B. Tachycardia
C. Peripheral edema
D. Decreased urine output
Question 1379: Which intervention is priority for hypoglycemia?
A. Administer insulin
B. Provide oral glucose
C. Encourage fluids
D. Monitor later
Question 1380: Which finding suggests hypoxia?
A. Pink skin
B. Restlessness
C. Bradycardia
D. Warm extremities
